title: daucus ribeirensis
intro: Daucus ribeirensis, also known as Ribeiro's carrot or ribeirense carrot, is a species of flowering plant in the carrot family (Apiaceae). It is native to Brazil and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. Daucus ribeirensis can be used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses.
